notes: Direct WebFetch to every vendor blog, aggregator, the HN Algolia API, and youtube.com (including yt-dlp's own CONNECT, tested directly) returned 403 at the proxy layer today — consistent with the known cloud-sandbox pattern described in SKILL.md. WebSearch was the sole discovery channel end-to-end. That's the large majority of configured sources blocked at the transport level; treated as expected environment degradation rather than a quiet-day signal. News: only 2 genuinely new stories survived a full pass over all 7 models, 4 aggregator channels, HN, and 3 broadened queries — this looks like a real quiet week for product news (dominated by the OpenAI/Hugging Face story), not under-searching. Expert content: min_expert_total (5) was NOT met — 3 published (1 Simon Willison + 2 broadened: Zvi Mowshowitz, Gary Marcus) against a target of 5, despite widening past the 8 configured experts. Of the 8 configured experts, only Simon Willison yielded a fresh, on-topic, non-duplicate item this cycle; the other 7 (Karpathy, Latent Space, AI Explained, Matt Wolfe, Ethan Mollick, Nate Herk, Matt Pocock) had nothing that was simultaneously fresh (7-day window), non-duplicate, and not a pure news-recap — a genuine channel-access gap (YouTube RSS/yt-dlp CONNECT blocked, WebSearch indexing lag on this week's uploads), not a lack of trying. All 3 published expert pieces analyze the same OpenAI/Hugging Face incident from distinct angles (technical/security, alignment/eval-practice, policy/liability) rather than 3 unrelated topics — a deliberate choice reflecting what actually dominated this week's expert discourse, not a search shortcut.
